sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 19.44 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.19)

statement.latex: By definition a profinite group has finite rank at most$r$if every subgroup of it can be (topologically) generated by$r$elements. Suppose that for every element$g$of a profinite group$G$there is a closed subgroup$E_g$of finite rank such that for every$x G$all sufficiently long Engel commutators$[x,g, , g]$belong to$E_g$, that is, for every$x G$there is a positive integer$n(x,g)$such that$[x,g, , g...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
